summary: American novelist. Best known for My Antonia (Jim Burden, Bohemian Shimerda family, Nebraska), O Pioneers! (Alexandra Bergson, Carl Linstrum), Death Comes for the Archbishop (Jean Latour, Father Vaillant, New Mexico), The Song of the Lark (Thea Kronborg, opera), and A Lost Lady (Marian Forrester, Niel Herbert). Also wrote "Paul's Case" and "A Wagner Matinee."

works.name: My Antonia
works.description: Novel narrated by Jim Burden about his childhood friend Antonia Shimerda, a Bohemian immigrant in Nebraska.

clues.clue: Narrated by Jim Burden — reminisces about childhood with Bohemian girl from the Shimerda family in Nebraska
examples: narrated by Jim Burden, who reminisces about his childhood growing up in Nebraska with the title Bohemian girl

clues.clue: Ends: "whatever we had missed, we possessed together the precious, the incommunicable past"
examples: whatever we had missed, we possessed together the precious, the incommunicable past
Anton Cuzak marries Jim Burden's friend

works.name: O Pioneers!
works.description: Novel about Alexandra Bergson managing the family farm in Nebraska. Part of the Great Plains trilogy.

clues.clue: Carl Linstrum marries Alexandra Bergson after death of Emil
examples: Carl Linstrum marries Alexandra Bergson after the death of Emil
the dedicatee of Willa Cather's O, Pioneers!

works.name: Death Comes for the Archbishop
works.description: Novel about Jean Latour and Father Vaillant setting up a diocese in New Mexico. Kit Carson appears.

mules Contento and Angelica serve the protagonist

works.name: The Song of the Lark
works.description: Bildungsroman about Thea Kronborg rising from Moonstone, Colorado to opera stardom.

clues.clue: Thea Kronborg moves from Moonstone, Colorado to become an opera singer — takes lessons with Harsanyi; falls in love with Fred Ottenburg at Flagstaff pueblos
examples: Thea Kronberg rises to opera stardom
falls in love with Fred Ottenburg while on a trip to the pueblos in Flagstaff

clues.clue: Thea rejects Ottenburg's proposal after discovering he has an invalid wife
examples: rejects Fred Ottenburg's proposal because of the discovery that he already has an invalid wife

works.name: A Lost Lady
works.description: Novel set in Sweet Water tracing Marian Forrester's decline as seen by narrator Niel Herbert.

clues.clue: Set in Sweet Water — Niel Herbert narrates the decline of Captain Forrester's wife Marian; she has affair with Frank Ellinger; leaves estate to Ivy Peters
examples: traces Neil Herbert's study of the decline of the title wife of Captain Forrester
leaves the management of her estate to Ivy Peters while she has an affair with Frank Ellinger

works.name: Shadows on the Rock
works.description: Novel set in Quebec. Hunchbacked Blinker tortures a woman; dying Count de Frontenac gifts glass fruit to protagonist.

clues.clue: Hunchbacked Blinker tortures woman; Count de Frontenac gifts glass fruit to protagonist, daughter of Quebecois apothecary
examples: a hunchbacked man named Blinker admits to torturing a woman
the dying Count de Frontenac gifts a glass fruit to the protagonist

works.name: Paul's Case
works.description: Short story about a boy who steals $1,000 and jumps under a train. From The Troll Garden.

clues.clue: Boy wears red carnation, ushers at Carnegie Hall; steals $1,000 from Pittsburgh bank, spends weekend in New York, jumps under a train
examples: a boy angers his teachers by wearing a red carnation and works as an usher at Carnegie Hall

works.name: A Wagner Matinee
works.description: Short story about Aunt Georgiana crying "I don't want to go" after hearing a symphony.

clues.clue: Aunt Georgiana cries "I don't want to go" after hearing a symphony — watches audience "like the granite Ramses in a museum"
examples: Aunt Georgiana cries 'I don't want to go' after hearing a symphony

works.name: Sapphira and the Slave Girl
works.description: Novel in which a woman suspects her husband of having an affair with Nancy, who escapes to Canada.

clues.clue: Woman suspects husband of affair with Nancy; Nancy escapes to Canada
examples: a woman suspects her husband of having an affair with Nancy, who then escapes to Canada

comprehensive_summary:
Willa Cather is an American novelist best known for her Great Plains trilogy and other works set in the American West. My Antonia is narrated by Jim Burden, who reminisces about his Nebraska childhood with the title Bohemian girl from the Shimerda family; the novel ends "whatever we had missed, we possessed together the precious, the incommunicable past." O Pioneers! follows Alexandra Bergson; Carl Linstrum marries her after Emil's death.

Death Comes for the Archbishop follows Jean Latour and Father Vaillant setting up a diocese in New Mexico, with Kit Carson as a character and mules named Contento and Angelica. The Song of the Lark traces Thea Kronborg's rise from Moonstone, Colorado to opera stardom; she takes lessons with Harsanyi and falls in love with Fred Ottenburg at the Flagstaff pueblos. A Lost Lady is set in Sweet Water, where narrator Niel Herbert watches the decline of Captain Forrester's wife Marian.

"Paul's Case" (from The Troll Garden) follows a boy who wears a red carnation, ushers at Carnegie Hall, steals $1,000, and jumps under a train. "A Wagner Matinee" depicts Aunt Georgiana crying "I don't want to go" after a symphony. Shadows on the Rock is set in Quebec, and Sapphira and the Slave Girl involves a woman suspecting her husband of an affair with Nancy.
